docstring:
/**
 * @brief Program entry point: prints two greetings, then totals argv lengths.
 *
 * Constructs a (currently unrecoverable) 22-byte greeting std::string and
 * invokes HelloWorld::printMessage() on a first instance. It then copies the
 * argv array into a std::vector<std::string> (reserving @p argc slots up
 * front), summing the lengths of every argument. After building an
 * 11-character "Sum printer" label, it invokes HelloWorld::printMessage() on
 * a second instance and writes two lines to std::cout:
 *   - "Total argument length: " followed by the accumulated length.
 *   - "Counter value: " followed by a counter whose source could not be
 *     recovered from the decompilation (emitted as 0 here).
 *
 * @param argc Number of entries in @p argv; used both as the loop bound and
 *             as the initial capacity reserved in the argument vector.
 * @param argv Array of NUL-terminated argument strings. Each entry is copied
 *             into a std::string; a NULL entry triggers the
 *             "basic_string::_M_construct null not valid" logic_error path.
 *
 * @return Always 0 on normal completion.
 * @retval 0 Success.
 *
 * @note May throw std::logic_error ("basic_string::_M_construct null not
 *       valid") if any argv[i] is NULL, and std::length_error ("cannot
 *       create std::vector larger than max_size()") if @p argc exceeds
 *       std::vector<std::string>::max_size().
 * @note The first greeting string and the "Counter value:" counter source
 *       were not fully recoverable from the binary; the reconstruction is
 *       approximate.
 *
 * @code
 * // Typical invocation from the OS loader:
 * //   $ ./prog one two three
 * // Output (schematically):
 * //   <greeting>
 * //   <greeting>
 * //   Total argument length: 17
 * //   Counter value: 0
 * @endcode
 */
assumptions: Tagged language=c per the prototype request, but emitted C++ syntax because the binary clearly uses libstdc++ (std::vector<std::string>, std::cout, std::endl, mangled HelloWorld::printMessage, std::string ctor/dtor at 0x12a0/0x1260).
Two HelloWorld objects (hello1, hello2) inferred from two printMessage() calls and two matching ctor/dtor pairs in the prologue/epilogue.

Replaced the inlined argv copy loop (L_1553..L_15d0) with reserve()+emplace_back; the binary inlines std::string's SSO branch (len<=15 stores into the embedded 16-byte buffer) and the heap branch (operator new at 0x1310, memcpy at 0x1220).
Replaced the 32-byte-stride accumulation loop (L_15d0..L_15ed) with a range-for summing s.size(); var8 is the running total printed after LBL_TOTAL_ARG_LENGTH.
Second std::string built from 11-byte literal 'Sum printer' decoded from immediates 0x6e697270206d7553 / 0x6574 / 0x72.

First std::string's first 8 bytes come from rodata[0x20a0] which is not recoverable from the pseudocode; left as a placeholder memcpy of an empty buffer with a comment. This is materially different from the original payload.
Counter value (stack_21) source could not be traced; emitted as a literal 0 placeholder.
Dropped duplicated basic-block tails (L_1508 dup, L_1538 dup of L_1530, L_16a4 dup of fallthrough) which are optimizer-emitted loop-peel artefacts.

Dropped the unreachable throw paths for ERR_VECTOR_EXCEEDS_MAX_SIZE; kept a single null-pointer check throwing ERR_STRING_NULL_NOT_VALID since the original branches to that string when val==NULL at L_1799.
reserve(argc) inferred from the (cursor_span/8) * 32 allocation pattern at 0x1250 (operator new) before the copy loop.

Renamed locals per the table where they map cleanly; some renames (val, obj, buf, cursor) collide across scopes in the pseudocode and were given role-appropriate names (greeting, hello1, args, sum_label) instead of mechanical substitution.

Did not reproduce the OOB write to buf[0x16] on a 16-byte stack array; the underlying buffer is the std::string's allocated storage, not a fixed-size stack array, so writing the NUL at offset len is correct in the rewrite.
divergences: [high] other: First std::string's leading 8 bytes are sourced from *(uint64_t*)&rodata[0x20a0] in the original, but the rewrite substitutes an empty buffer placeholder. The constructed string content is materially different.
[high] changed_constant: Original prints stack_21 (some traced counter value) after 'Counter value: '. Rewrite hard-codes 0, changing observable program output.

[medium] other: Rewrite uses undefined symbolic identifiers instead of the actual string literals ("Total argument length: ", "Counter value: ", "basic_string::_M_construct null not valid") shown in the pseudocode. Code will not compile and observable strings are not preserved verbatim.

[medium] dropped_call: Original calls 0x1210("basic_string::_M_construct null not valid") then 0x1270() then 0x11f0("cannot create std::vector larger than max_size()"). Rewrite throws a single logic_error and drops the std::length_error / __throw_length_error path entirely.

[medium] dropped_call: Original computes (cursor_span >> 3) and checks against 0x1ffffffffffffff8, throwing 'cannot create std::vector larger than max_size()' on overflow before calling operator new. Rewrite's reserve() does not reproduce this explicit overflow-throw branch with the original message.
[low] dropped_side_effect: Compiler-inserted stack canary save/check is dropped. Standard for source rewrites; not a behavioral change at the C++ source level.
[low] control_flow: Inlined SSO/heap branch loop (L_1530/L_1538/L_1553/L_15ae) collapsed to emplace_back. Semantically equivalent assuming libstdc++ std::string ctor.
[low] control_flow: 32-byte-stride accumulation collapsed to range-for. Semantically equivalent.
[low] other: Rewrite writes NUL at offset 0x16 in a 24-byte stack buffer; safe because tmp[24]. Original wrote NUL at len offset of std::string storage. Behavior equivalent for assign().
[low] added_call: An explicit reserve() call is introduced; original directly invokes operator new for argc*32 bytes. Source-level effect equivalent.
[low] other: Duplicated basic-block tails in the pseudocode are loop-peel/optimizer artefacts; the rewriter correctly collapsed them.
[low] sign_extension: Original used movsxd on argc (sign-extend 32->64) before shifting. Rewriter's static_cast<size_t>(argc) on a positive int is equivalent.
